title: matelea altamirana
intro: Matelea altamirana, a species of the Asclepiadaceae family, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Matelea altamirana include the potential treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever reduction. In herbal preparations, Matelea altamirana is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a paste or salve.
